title: parse_proxy_addr: IPv6 proxy addresses not handled
description:
parse_proxy_addr uses rsplit_once(':') to split host and port. For an IPv6 proxy URL such as socks5://[::1]:9050:

1. After stripping the 'socks5://' prefix, addr = '[::1]:9050'
2. rsplit_once(':') gives host='[::1]', port_str='9050'
3. The brackets are NOT stripped, so tokio-socks receives '[::1]' as the proxy host

Whether tokio-socks handles bracketed IPv6 addresses is untested. In most TCP resolver implementations, '[::1]' (with brackets) is not a valid hostname and will fail to connect with an opaque error.

No validation rejects or warns about IPv6 addresses, so a user who configures socks5://[::1]:9050 gets a silent connect failure rather than a clear 'IPv6 proxy addresses are not supported' error.

File: core/src/transport.rs, function parse_proxy_addr (~line 423).
